ticket.subject: Welcome to Shopify Partner PluginHive! We’re here to help you save both time and money
ticket.raw_subject: Welcome to Shopify Partner PluginHive! We’re here to help you save both time and money
ticket.description:
Hello Anandhaas,

 Greeting from Shopify Partner PluginHive!

 Thank you for installing our Shopify App - **“PH Multi Carrier Shipping Label App”** on shree-anandhaas-sweets-snacks.myshopify.com

 Our team would like to assist you with the complete app onboarding.

 We request you let us know a suitable time for the onboarding session, which will not take more than 10 mins. Feel free to reply to this email (or) share your contact number, we will get in touch with you.

 You can also refer our documentation to set up the app and start shipping - click here to know more

 Looking forward to hearing from you.

 Thanks and Regards,
 Team PluginHive
https://www.pluginhive.com/about/#team

comments.body:
L3: https://pluginhive.slack.com/archives/C02E15X8X0C/p1778150370601819

MCSL customer reached out stating that the error message shown during label failures for Amazon shipments is unclear and causing confusion.
Upon checking other stores, the usual error message displayed is:
“No offerings found for the given request. Either it is out of coverage or dimensions or weight are not supported.”

However, for this particular customer, the error being displayed is:
“No Shipping Service selected. Please check your Automation Rules.”

This appears to be misleading, as the case seems related to Amazon coverage/service availability rather than automation rules.

Could you please check why a different error message is being displayed for this store and update accordingly?

Store URL : shree-anandhaas-sweets-snacks.myshopify.com
AC ID: 059270bd-5882-4ba7-b27b-05198f532b12

I have attached the pictures for reference. Posting after discussing with Keerthi.
comments.html_body:
L3: https://pluginhive.slack.com/archives/C02E15X8X0C/p1778150370601819
 
MCSL customer reached out stating that the error message shown during label failures for Amazon shipments is unclear and causing confusion.
Upon checking other stores, the usual error message displayed is:
“No offerings found for the given request. Either it is out of coverage or dimensions or weight are not supported.”
 
However, for this particular customer, the error being displayed is:
“No Shipping Service selected. Please check your Automation Rules.”
 
This appears to be misleading, as the case seems related to Amazon coverage/service availability rather than automation rules.
 
Could you please check why a different error message is being displayed for this store and update accordingly?
 
Store URL : shree-anandhaas-sweets-snacks.myshopify.com
AC ID: 059270bd-5882-4ba7-b27b-05198f532b12
 
I have attached the pictures for reference. Posting after discussing with Keerthi.

comments.body:
*Ticket is already in dev_needed for error mapping*

L3 - Customer is requesting to see the Estimated Delivery Days in the All Orders page and Open Orders page that is provided by Amazon Shipping carrier

Currently, the delivery days can be seen only in the order summary page

If this is shown in all orders page and open orders page, during the label generation, the customer will look into the delivery days provided for each order and use the specific service for the label generation

Please check this requirement and provide an update for the same

Request has come through Phone Call

comments.body:
Hi Team,

Thank you so much for your time over the call.

As discussed, you can create automation rules based on zones and assign the Amazon Express service to specific pincodes.

We would request you to please reach out to the Amazon team and obtain the required pincodes. Once you have them, please share the details with us, and we will be happy to help you set up the automation rules accordingly.

Regarding the Estimated Delivery Days display on the All Orders page, our team is currently enhancing the app for this functionality. This is expected to be available by the first week of June, and we will keep you updated on the progress.

Please feel free to reach out if you need any further assistance.

Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hi Team,
 
Thank you so much for your time over the call.
 
As discussed, you can create automation rules based on zones and assign the Amazon Express service to specific pincodes.
 
We would request you to please reach out to the Amazon team and obtain the required pincodes. Once you have them, please share the details with us, and we will be happy to help you set up the automation rules accordingly.
 
Regarding the Estimated Delivery Days display on the All Orders page, our team is currently enhancing the app for this functionality. This is expected to be available by the first week of June, and we will keep you updated on the progress.
 
Please feel free to reach out if you need any further assistance.
 
Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive
